# TrueVector India Partners

> Specialist advisory firm helping foreign companies establish, operate, and stay fully compliant in India. End-to-end support across company incorporation, statutory compliance, transfer pricing, GST, accounting, payroll, and FEMA / RBI matters — designed specifically for foreign-owned Indian subsidiaries. Based in Mumbai, India.

TrueVector India Partners (CIN U70200MR2026PTC475960) is a Mumbai-headquartered advisory firm founded in 2026 by two senior finance professionals who have spent over two decades working with foreign companies entering and operating in India. The firm positions itself in the mid-market segment — priced below large compliance firms (which typically charge USD 50,000 to USD 90,000 for equivalent Year 1 scope) and a meaningful premium over discount online portals — delivering genuine compliance depth at a price point that works for growing foreign subsidiaries.

The firm's three core values are: **Compliance First** (prioritise getting it right before getting it done); **Honest Counsel** (tell clients what they need to hear, not what they want to hear); **Complete Transparency** (fixed fees, documented scope, no surprises).

## Target Audience

- CFOs, Finance Directors, and Heads of Tax at foreign companies planning India entry
- Founders and CEOs of small to mid-market foreign companies (50 to 500 employees globally, revenue USD 10M to USD 200M)
- Group HQ teams at multinationals managing or planning Indian subsidiaries
- Companies headquartered in Western Europe (UK, Germany, Netherlands, France, Switzerland), the US, Israel, Australia, Japan, and South Korea
- Key sectors served: IT and technology, manufacturing, pharma and life sciences, fintech, professional services

## Services

- [India Market Entry](https://www.truevectorindia.com/): Company incorporation (Private Limited, LLP, Branch / Liaison / Project Office), tax and regulatory registrations (PAN, TAN, GST, PT, IEC), nominee directors, registered address
- [Statutory Compliance and Accounting](https://www.truevectorindia.com/): Income tax, GST, TDS, ROC, statutory audit coordination, FEMA / RBI compliance, monthly accounting and MIS reporting, transfer pricing documentation and Form 3CEB
- [Payroll and HR](https://www.truevectorindia.com/): Monthly payroll processing, statutory deductions (PF, ESI, PT), Form 16 issuance, expat and inpat tax services
- [Corporate Finance Advisory](https://www.truevectorindia.com/): Capital structure, External Commercial Borrowing (ECB), intercompany pricing, repatriation planning

## Insights — India Entry Series

A six-part series for foreign CFOs, founders, and finance leaders evaluating or actively pursuing India entry.

- [India Entry for Foreign Companies in 2026: The Five Decisions That Actually Matter](https://www.truevectorindia.com/insights/india-entry-five-decisions-2026/): Overview of the five decisions that matter in any India entry — entity vs no entity, entity structure, location, capital and funding, and compliance ownership. Published 22 June 2026.
- [Decision One: Do You Actually Need an Entity in India Yet?](https://www.truevectorindia.com/insights/india-entry-decision-one-do-you-need-entity/): Compares Employer of Record (EOR), SNRR account, and own entity routes. Explains when each is right, the EOR-to-entity cross-over (typically 5 to 8 employees), permanent establishment risk, and the two expensive mistakes most foreign companies make. Published 27 June 2026.

## Upcoming Insights (Publishing through 2026)

- Decision Two: Which Entity Structure — Pvt Ltd, LLP, Branch, Liaison, or Project Office
- Decision Three: Where to Set Up — Choosing the Right Indian City for Operations
- Decision Four: Capital and Funding Structure — Equity, ECB, or Intercompany Credit
- Decision Five: Who Owns Day-to-Day Compliance — Local CFO, Big Firm, or Outsourced Specialist

## Key Regulatory Topics

- Permanent Establishment (PE) risk for foreign companies operating in India
- FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act) and RBI compliance for foreign-owned subsidiaries
- Goods and Services Tax (GST) for service exports, inter-state supply, and import of services
- Transfer pricing and Form 3CEB for intercompany transactions
- Tax Deducted at Source (TDS), including Section 195 on payments to foreign companies
- FEMA reporting: FC-GPR, FC-TRS, FLA, ODI, External Commercial Borrowings (ECB)
- ROC (Registrar of Companies) annual filings: AOC-4, MGT-7, DIR-3 KYC, INC-22A
- Statutory audit thresholds and timing
- Compliance calendar for foreign-owned Indian subsidiaries
